failures.phase4_evidence: All nine A_task_only prompt fixtures included evaluator/status fields outside the Phase 1 A-condition allowed payload.
failures.effect: A_task_only rows are preserved as raw responses but are not valid clean-baseline evidence; the A/B/C comparison cannot support a usefulness promotion.
failures.required_followup: Before any new response collection, obtain explicit human approval for repaired prompt count, response-subject surface, retry policy, and artifact paths.

failures.phase4_evidence: The approved collection used one downstream-agent response per prompt.
failures.effect: Even after prompt repair, a single response per prompt is diagnostic and cannot establish general downstream-agent reliability.
failures.required_followup: If stronger claims are desired, predeclare replicated response-subject sampling, randomization/counterbalancing, and uncertainty reporting before collection.

failures.phase4_evidence: Prompt collection followed manifest order, with later prompts collected in small batches as agent capacity allowed.
failures.effect: Order effects cannot be ruled out.
failures.required_followup: Use randomized or counterbalanced prompt order in any new approved collection.

failures.phase4_evidence: B and C rows passed all required dimensions and tied numerically under the frozen rubric.
failures.effect: C_human_framed superiority over B_evidence_only is not established; the benchmark may need more discriminating cases or scoring dimensions before it can measure framing benefit.
failures.required_followup: Design harder cases or predeclared discriminability checks without changing Phase 4 scoring post hoc.

failures.phase4_evidence: Subagent identifiers for the first thirteen response artifacts were not preserved in local files after reboot/context compaction.
failures.effect: Raw response artifacts and no-retry ledger remain preserved, but early response-subject identity is incomplete.
failures.required_followup: Future collection manifests should record subagent id/nickname immediately after each response is saved.

non_claims: This taxonomy does not establish downstream-agent usefulness.
This taxonomy does not validate a repaired benchmark result.
This taxonomy does not authorize new response collection.
This taxonomy does not support release, public benchmark, scientific, product, broad theorem-proving, or general model-reliability claims.
